steps.title: Fastest path: mark the given information first
steps.content: Mark the given angle relationships first: equal angles, supplementary angles, parallel-line angles, and triangle sums of $180^\circ$. Most of these questions become a short angle chase once the givens are marked.

steps.title: Apply AA similarity criterion
steps.content: We have $$\angle A = \angle P = 62^{\circ}$$. To prove triangle similarity with minimal additional information, we use the AA criterion-we need just one more pair of equal angles.

steps.title: Evaluate the candidate
steps.content:
Given $$\angle B = 40^{\circ}$$ and $$\angle R = 78^{\circ}$$:

In triangle ABC: $$\angle C = 180^{\circ} - 62^{\circ} - 40^{\circ} = 78^{\circ}$$
In triangle PQR: $$\angle Q = 180^{\circ} - 62^{\circ} - 78^{\circ} = 40^{\circ}$$
Result:
  • $$\angle A = \angle P = 62^{\circ}$$
  • $$\angle B = \angle Q = 40^{\circ}$$
  • $$\angle C = \angle R = 78^{\circ}$$
All three angles match across the correspondence A to P, B to Q, C to R. That matches the correct answer - C is correct.

steps.title: Verify other choices fail
steps.content: The measures of angle B and angle Q are 62 degrees and 40 degrees, respectively. creates angles 62 degrees, 62 degrees, 56 degrees in ABC but 62 degrees, 40 degrees, 78 degrees in PQR-no correspondence exists. Two other options lack the proportionality $$\frac{AB}{AC} = \frac{PQ}{PR}$$ that SAS similarity requires.
